settings.title: Primary bath, down to the studs
settings.summary: A leaking 1980s bathroom stripped to the framing and rebuilt with a freestanding tub, walk-in shower, and heated tile floor.
settings.description:
What looked like a regrout job turned out to be a slow leak that had been working on the subfloor for years. We opened the room to the studs, replaced the damaged framing, and rebuilt from the waterproofing outward.

The new layout trades a cramped corner shower and unused garden tub for a proper walk-in shower and a freestanding soaker under the window. Heated floors, a curbless entry, and blocking in the walls for future grab bars — the invisible work that makes a bathroom last decades, not years.

The owners kept use of the hall bath for all six weeks. Final invoice matched the signed quote, including the framing repair we priced as a change order in week one.
